summary: Russia launched a large-scale overnight attack on Ukraine using 2 anti-ship missiles, 6 ballistic missiles, and 142 drones, with strikes hitting 11 locations. Simultaneously, Ukrainian forces deployed Shahed-136-type drones from multiple directions across Kharkiv, Dnipropetrovsk, Donetsk, Zaporizhzhia, Chernihiv, and Kirovograd oblasts. Russia also struck Zaporizhzhia with guided bombs, injuring at least 8 people including two children, one in serious condition.
key_points: Russia attacked Ukraine overnight with 2 anti-ship missiles (Oniks/Zircon), 6 ballistic missiles (Iskander-M/S-400), and 142 drones, with impacts at 11 locations and debris falls at 13 others (Source: @UkraineNow/82232).
Russia struck Zaporizhzhia with guided bombs (KABs), injuring at least 8 people including a 5-year-old boy in serious condition (Source: @UkraineNow/82244).
Ukrainian Shahed-136-type drones were launched from the Kursk direction in approximately 20 groups, with additional launches from the Millerovo area in about 6 groups (Source: @eRadarrua/98563).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs (KABs) on Donetsk and Kharkiv oblasts (Source: @kpszsu/66685).

Ukrainian drones were tracked across multiple oblasts: Kharkiv (toward Dergachi/Mala Danylivka, near Staryi Merchik, near Malynivka), Dnipropetrovsk (toward Pavlohrad, Bozhedarivka, past Sofiivka toward Kirovograd), Donetsk (toward Dobropillia/Bilozerske), Zaporizhzhia (toward Balabine, from the south), Chernihiv (toward Pryluky, Korop, Horodnia, Kholmy, Novhorod-Siverskyi, Varva, Snovsk), and Kirovograd (reactive drone from Mykolaiv region) (Source: @kpszsu/66703).
All districts of Kherson oblast are fully or partially without power, according to the regional governor (Source: @tass_agency/382850).
Russian forces are completing the assault on Konstantinovka in DPR, with encircled and disorganized AFU units remaining in the city (Source: @tass_agency/382845).
Ukraine will require nearly $146 billion in external financing for 2026–2029, Finance Minister Marchenko announced at a forum in Gdansk (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31224).

sections.executive_summary: Russia conducted a large-scale combined overnight strike on Ukraine employing 2 anti-ship missiles, 6 ballistic missiles, and 142 drones, with confirmed impacts at 11 locations. Ukrainian forces simultaneously launched waves of Shahed-136-type drones from Kursk and Millerovo directions against Russian rear areas. The Russian assault on Konstantinovka in DPR is nearing completion, with encircled AFU units described as disorganized.

points: Ukraine's Air Force reported that Russia launched 2 anti-ship missiles (Oniks/Zircon), 6 ballistic missiles (Iskander-M/S-400), and 142 drones in an overnight attack, with missile and drone strikes hitting 11 locations and debris from intercepted targets falling at 13 additional locations (Source: @UkraineNow/82232).

Ukrainian Shahed-136-type drones were launched from the Kursk direction in approximately 20 groups, with additional launches from the Millerovo area in about 6 groups, targeting Russian rear infrastructure (Source: @eRadarrua/98563).

Ukrainian drones were tracked across multiple axes: Kharkiv oblast toward Dergachi/Mala Danylivka and near Staryi Merchik and Malynivka; Dnipropetrovsk oblast toward Pavlohrad, Bozhedarivka, and past Sofiivka toward Kirovograd; Donetsk oblast toward Dobropillia/Bilozerske; Zaporizhzhia oblast toward Balabine and from the south; Chernihiv oblast toward Pryluky, Korop, Horodnia, Kholmy, Novhorod-Siverskyi, Varva, and Snovsk (Source: @kpszsu/66703).

Finance Minister Sergey Marchenko announced Ukraine will require nearly $146 billion in external financing for 2026–2029 following meetings with UK and Lithuanian counterparts at a forum in Gdansk (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31224).
Mobilization enforcement continues in Ukraine, with footage from Lutsk showing a man being pushed into a van while city residents attempted unsuccessfully to free him (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31220).

Moldovan President Maia Sandu stated that the withdrawal of Russian troops from Transnistria could become part of EU-mediated peace talks on Ukraine, calling the illegal Russian military presence the region's main problem (Source: @UkraineNow/82242).
Russia struck Zaporizhzhia with guided aviation bombs (KABs), injuring at least 8 people including two children, with a 5-year-old boy reported in serious condition (Source: @UkraineNow/82244).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs (KABs) on targets in Donetsk and Kharkiv oblasts (Source: @kpszsu/66685).
The Russian Defense Ministry claimed that 209 Ukrainian drones were shot down over Russian regions overnight (Source: @tass_agency/382862).

Putin stated in an interview that Russia was asked to make compromises formulated by American negotiators during the Alaska talks, and that Russia expects US negotiators to visit after the completion of the 'hot phase' of events around Iran (Source: @tass_agency/382826).

Putin acknowledged that Ukrainian strikes on Russian energy infrastructure are creating problems, though Kremlin officials claim the damage is 'not critical' and that all damaged facilities are quickly restored; Crimea's fuel reserves are said to last several days with supply to be strengthened by land and sea (Source: @eRadarrua/98558).
Traffic across the Kerch Bridge was temporarily suspended, according to the situation monitoring channel (Source: @tass_agency/382857).

A Russian national from Kursk region returned from Ukrainian captivity alleged that AFU soldiers burned the letter 'Z' on his arm while he was in a field hospital in Sumy oblast (Source: @tass_agency/382860).

Russian forces are completing the heavy, months-long assault on Konstantinovka in DPR, with the commander of the 1st battalion of the 1465th MSP (4th Brigade, Southern Grouping) reporting that encircled AFU units in the city are disorganized (Source: @tass_agency/382845).

Russian forces are isolating and encircling Krasny Lyman and leveling the front on the Slavyansk direction, with a concentration of forces visible for the upcoming battle for the Slavyansk-Kramatorsk agglomeration (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31218).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs (KABs) on Donetsk oblast (Source: @kpszsu/66685).
Ukrainian drones were tracked over Donetsk oblast moving toward Dobropillia/Bilozerske (Source: @kpszsu/66717).
Moscow is postulating the liberation of Donbas from AFU as a condition for entering the peace track and as the last unsequestered goal of the SVO (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31218).

Ukrainian drones were active across Chernihiv oblast on multiple axes: from Dmytrivka toward Pryluky, near Kholmy and Ponornytsia, toward Korop, Horodnia, Novhorod-Siverskyi, Varva, Snovsk, and Koriukivka (Source: @eRadarrua/98568).
Six Ukrainian drones were tracked circling in the Kholmy and Ponornytsia area of Chernihiv oblast, with additional drones near Kholmy and Semenivka (Source: @eRadarrua/98532).
Ukrainian drones in southern Chernihiv oblast were heading toward Poltava oblast (Pyratyyn) (Source: @kpszsu/66695).
Ukrainian drones were also tracked over Sumy oblast near Pischane (Source: @eRadarrua/98577).
A Russian national returned from captivity alleged abuse at a prison in Vinnytsia, claiming convoys beat prisoners two to three times daily (Source: @tass_agency/382847).
Russia struck Zaporizhzhia with guided bombs (KABs), injuring at least 8 people including two children, one in serious condition (Source: @UkraineNow/82244).

Ukrainian drones were tracked over Zaporizhzhia oblast heading toward the city of Zaporizhzhia and Balabine from the north, with additional drones approaching from the south and a reactive drone in the Nikopol area heading toward Apostolove (Source: @kpszsu/66712).
All districts of Kherson oblast are fully or partially without power, according to the regional governor (Source: @tass_agency/382850).
A reactive Ukrainian drone was tracked on the border of Mykolaiv and Dnipropetrovsk oblasts heading northwest (Source: @kpszsu/66688).
Russian forces in Konstantinovka (DPR, southern grouping) are completing the assault on the city with encircled AFU units (Source: @tass_agency/382845).

Russia launched 2 anti-ship missiles (Oniks/Zircon), 6 ballistic missiles (Iskander-M/S-400), and 142 drones in an overnight combined attack on Ukraine, with 1 confirmed missile hit and 14 strike drone hits across 11 locations (Source: @UkraineNow/82232).
Ukrainian Shahed-136-type drones were launched from the Kursk direction in approximately 20 groups, with additional launches from Millerovo in about 6 groups (Source: @eRadarrua/98563).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs (KABs) on Donetsk and Kharkiv oblasts (Source: @kpszsu/66685).
A ballistic weapons threat was declared, indicating potential use of additional ballistic missile systems (Source: @kpszsu/66708).
The Russian Defense Ministry claimed 209 Ukrainian drones were shot down over Russian regions overnight (Source: @tass_agency/382862).

Ukrainian drones were tracked across at least 8 oblasts: Kharkiv, Dnipropetrovsk, Donetsk, Zaporizhzhia, Chernihiv, Kirovograd, Sumy, and Mykolaiv, indicating a broad multi-axis drone campaign (Source: @kpszsu/66703).

key_developments: Russia conducted a massive overnight combined strike with 2 anti-ship missiles, 6 ballistic missiles, and 142 drones across Ukraine, with impacts at 11 locations and debris at 13 others (Source: @UkraineNow/82232).

Ukrainian Shahed-136-type drones launched from Kursk (~20 groups) and Millerovo (~6 groups) directions in a coordinated multi-axis strike campaign against Russian rear areas (Source: @eRadarrua/98563).
Russia struck Zaporizhzhia with guided bombs, injuring at least 8 including a 5-year-old boy in serious condition (Source: @UkraineNow/82244).
Russian forces are completing the assault on Konstantinovka in DPR, with encircled AFU units described as disorganized by a Russian battalion commander (Source: @tass_agency/382845).
All districts of Kherson oblast are fully or partially de-energized, according to the regional governor (Source: @tass_agency/382850).
Ukraine will require nearly $146 billion in external financing for 2026–2029, Finance Minister Marchenko announced at a Gdansk forum (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31224).

Putin acknowledged Ukrainian strikes on Russian energy infrastructure are creating problems, though Kremlin claims damage is non-critical; Crimea fuel reserves said to last several days (Source: @eRadarrua/98558).
sections.threat_assessment: The operational tempo is intensifying on both sides, with Russia conducting large-scale combined missile-drone strikes and Ukraine responding with multi-axis Shahed-type drone campaigns across at least 8 oblasts. The Russian ground offensive in DPR is reaching a critical phase with the near-complete assault on Konstantinovka and preparations for the Slavyansk-Kramatorsk agglomeration battle. The threat of further ballistic missile use remains elevated, as indicated by active ballistic weapons declarations. Russia's energy infrastructure remains vulnerable to Ukrainian strikes, while Ukrainian civilian infrastructure continues to suffer from Russian missile and guided bomb attacks.
sections.regional_response: Moldovan President Maia Sandu raised the issue of Russian troop withdrawal from Transnistria as a potential component of EU-mediated peace talks on Ukraine. Latvia and Ukraine announced plans to build a drone factory on the border with Russia and Belarus. Former US President Biden criticized Trump for undermining NATO, claiming he 'chooses Putin over American allies.' China-Belarus relations reached a 'historical peak' according to Xi Jinping during a meeting with Lukashenko in Beijing. The EU is reportedly shifting defense strategy toward mass production of cheaper strike systems including drones, missiles, and guided bombs.
sections.intelligence_notes: The source 'Two Majors' report indicates Moscow views the liberation of Donbas as a precondition for peace talks, suggesting no near-term diplomatic resolution. Russian claims of 209 Ukrainian drones intercepted overnight should be treated as unverified. The Kerch Bridge traffic suspension may indicate either a security threat or infrastructure issue requiring monitoring. Putin's separate statements on Alaska compromises and energy infrastructure problems suggest the Kremlin is simultaneously managing diplomatic signaling and domestic damage control. The reported shift in EU defense strategy toward mass drone production could significantly alter the long-term balance of strike capabilities.
